name: Bard Academy
about: Bard Academy is a two-year (grades 9-10) boarding and day program at Bard College at Simon's Rock, located on Bard College's Massena Campus in the Hudson Valley. It is the nation's first two-year high school designed to prepare students to begin college early. After completing the 10th-grade program, students in good standing are admitted to Simon's Rock at Bard College to begin an associate degree, creating an accelerated pathway from high school into college.
college_matriculation: Students progress directly into Simon's Rock at Bard College for early college study; graduates of the broader Bard/Simon's Rock pathway transfer to selective four-year colleges.
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; in a typical year over 80% of students receive substantial financial assistance.

name: Darrow School
about: Darrow School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school in New Lebanon, New York, set on the historic Mount Lebanon Shaker Village, a National Historic Landmark. Founded in 1932, it serves grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year on a 365-acre campus in the Berkshire foothills. The school emphasizes hands-on learning and stewardship of its Shaker heritage. After nearly closing, it has stabilized and is expanding to a second campus.
college_matriculation: 100% attend four-year colleges; graduates have gone on to a range of US universities and colleges.
notable_alumni: Pete Conrad, Christopher Lloyd, Chris Russo, Sam Harper
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; at least 60% of students receive aid.
extracurriculars: Syracuse University Project Advance (SUPA) college credit courses

name: Education First EF Academy
about: EF Academy New York is an international boarding high school in Thornwood, New York, operated by Education First. It serves grades 9-12 on a 97-acre campus, drawing students from over 75 countries with a strongly international student body. The school offers both the US high school diploma and the International Baccalaureate (IB) diploma, alongside Advanced Placement courses.
college_matriculation: Graduates matriculate to a wide range of US and international universities.
financial_aid_notes: Merit-based scholarships and need-based aid available.

name: Emma Willard School
about: Emma Willard School is an independent university-preparatory boarding and day school for young women in Troy, New York, founded by women's rights advocate Emma Willard in 1814. Located on Mount Ida, it serves grades 9-12 plus postgraduate study and was the first institution for women's higher education in the United States. The school has replaced AP courses with its own Advanced Studies curriculum emphasizing inquiry and independent research.
college_matriculation: Graduates matriculate to selective colleges and universities nationwide.
notable_alumni: Elizabeth Cady Stanton, Jane Fonda, Kirsten Gillibrand, Eunice Newton Foote
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; 44% of students receive aid; ~$6.6M awarded; average boarding award ~$51,994.

name: Storm King School
about: The Storm King School is an independent coeducational boarding and day school in Cornwall-on-Hudson, New York, and one of the state's oldest boarding schools, founded in 1867. It serves grades 8-12 plus a postgraduate year and enrolls students from across the United States and more than 20 countries. The hillside campus overlooks the Hudson River, and the school offers a broad athletics program.
college_matriculation: Harvard, Yale, MIT, Columbia, Penn, Cornell, Carnegie Mellon, Georgetown, University of Chicago, Notre Dame, Tufts, Wellesley, UVA, West Point, NYU Stern

name: Gow School
about: The Gow School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school in South Wales, New York, near Buffalo. Founded in 1926, it is the nation's first school for students with dyslexia and other language-based learning differences, using the Orton-based Reconstructive Language program. It serves students roughly grades 5 through 12 plus postgraduate on a 100-acre campus with a 4:1 student-teacher ratio.
notable_alumni: Brooks McCabe, Quinn Bradlee, Brian Talma, Daniel B. Wesson II

name: Hackley School
about: Hackley School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory day school (with a small five-day boarding program for upper-school students) in Tarrytown, New York, founded in 1899 by philanthropist Frances Hackley. Originally affiliated with Unitarianism and all-boys, it is now secular and coeducational, serving grades K-12 on a hilltop estate campus overlooking the Hudson River. The school is known for academic rigor and a strong music tradition.
college_matriculation: Strong matriculation to Ivy League and other highly selective colleges and universities.
notable_alumni: Philip Johnson, George Hamilton, Keith Olbermann, Dara Khosrowshahi

name: Keio Academy of New York
about: Keio Academy of New York is a private, coeducational boarding and day high school in the Purchase area of Harrison, New York, founded in 1990. Sponsored by Keio University, it is the university's only overseas branch school and follows a bilingual Japanese-English program serving grades 9-12 on a 27-acre campus. Many graduates continue on to Keio University in Japan, while others matriculate to US universities.
college_matriculation: Many graduates proceed to Keio University in Japan; others attend Georgetown, Columbia, University of Tokyo, and Parsons School of Design.
accreditation: MSA; Japanese Ministry of Education (MEXT) recognized; State of New York
financial_aid_notes: Bursary program for families in need; sports scholarships for select 10th-11th graders.
